title: Dogfooding Robustness Followups
goal: Implement the open dogfooding issues created or surfaced around the generated CLI adapter lane: #645, #649, #650, #651, #652, #653, #654, and #655.
non_goals: Do not reopen the already-merged #641 generated-adapter foundation.
Do not promote mutation-capable lifecycle adapters without apply/verify proof.
Do not force execplans for narrow direct tasks.
Do not weaken archive or structured-file guardrails to hide closeout friction.

intent.outcome: Dogfooding findings become concrete workflow and contract robustness improvements rather than standing open issues.
intent.constraints: Use checked-in planning before broad implementation, keep package boundaries explicit, close issues only after local proof, and commit/push in bounded batches.
intent.latitude: Implement issues in dependency order; route any oversized or unsafe item into a narrower child issue if it cannot be responsibly completed in this lane.
intent.escalation: Stop before broad behavior rewrites, lifecycle mutation enablement, or weakening storage/checking policy.

execution.next_step: Commit and push the generated-output and conformance/modeling batch, then close the implemented dogfooding issues.

invariants: Workflow guards must funnel broad/high-assurance work into planning without blocking narrow direct work.
Generated adapter and operation parity checks must remain strict for the correct command surface.
Lifecycle maturity must separate dry-run/refusal proof from apply/mutation proof.
Archive guardrails must be enforced before a completed plan is written into historical audit storage.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Improve the package's ability to keep agents in the correct workflow and reduce repeated dogfooding friction.
intent_continuity.this slice completes the larger intended: yes if the named dogfooding issues are implemented or explicitly routed with narrower follow-ups.

required_continuation.activation trigger: A named issue proves too broad or requires behavior outside the bounded surfaces.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Implemented the workflow/archive robustness batch for #645, #649, #654, and #655.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: No named dogfooding issue remains intentionally deferred in this lane.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Archive-size guardrails will also block this execplan unless it is distilled before closeout.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Close implemented issues and archive this execplan after compacting it below archive guardrails.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Merged. Fix the dogfooding issues next.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Continue the post-merge dogfooding queue without losing the package workflow discipline that the previous lane established.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Use one active plan for the dogfooding follow-up lane and implement issues in bounded batches, closing each after proof.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Verify that fixes reduce workflow friction without adding heavyweight process for narrow work.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Planning/archive workflow code, summary/projection code, command/operation/conformance contracts, generated adapter checks, focused docs/reviews, tests, and this planning state.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Broad but bounded to the named dogfooding issues.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py tests/test_generated_tool_conformance.py tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; make check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before replacing the planning data model, changing package versioning, or enabling lifecycle mutation adapters.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated documentation cleanup, unrelated historical archive rewrites, unrelated CLI behavior changes.

stop_conditions.stop when: A fix would weaken a safety guard instead of improving the workflow.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A dogfooding issue needs a large architectural change that cannot be proved in the current lane.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation starts requiring unrelated package rewrites.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: make check or focused conformance reveals an invariant break that cannot be fixed within the issue scope.

context_budget.live working set: #645, #649, #650, #651, #652, #653, #654, #655, planning archive/summary code, operation/conformance contracts, generated checks.
context_budget.recoverable later: Historical #641 chat context and older dogfooding issues not named in this plan.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, drift_log, and issue comments before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: AGENTS.md and summary loaded; master synced.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Use compact memory routing only if issue implementation uncovers durable repo knowledge not represented by checks.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before leaving a partially implemented issue, record the exact failing check or next file to edit.

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Use package workflow, avoid skipping planning for broad work, close issues only after proof, commit and push as useful batches.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Issue order, batching, exact contract shape, and when a finding deserves a new issue.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A named issue cannot be implemented without weakening safety or expanding beyond the current lane.

adaptive_assurance.reason: The lane changes workflow guards, planning archive behavior, generated adapter contract checks, and lifecycle conformance semantics.

decision_refs: Implement workflow/archive robustness before deeper adapter namespace and lifecycle maturity expansion.

evidence: This checked-in execplan created before code edits.

control_gates.next_action: Run focused tests after each batch.

invariant_refs: No workflow guard may block narrow direct work.
Lifecycle mutation promotion remains deferred without apply proof.

forbidden: network-dependent runtime behavior beyond GitHub issue/PR metadata

common_risks: Making workflow heavier instead of clearer.
Hiding archive-size problems by loosening guardrails.
Conflating lifecycle dry-run maturity with mutation maturity.
Weakening root command parity while enabling package-local commands.
durable_residue_prompts: Record any new friction as narrow dogfooding issues.

decision_refs: Workflow/archive guardrails should be actionable at the point of failure.
architecture_decision_promotion.notes: A review note is enough unless the fixes change public command semantics.

threat_failure_aids.concern: The high-assurance guard could force plans for narrow work.
threat_failure_aids.advisory: Keep the guard scoped to broad/high-assurance or multi-surface package work.

threat_failure_aids.concern: Archive-size fixes could weaken historical audit storage policy.
threat_failure_aids.advisory: Enforce guardrails before write; do not raise limits as the default fix.

active_milestone.optional_deps: Node for generated adapter conformance if #651 is implemented in this lane.

immediate_next_action: Commit and push the generated-output and conformance/modeling batch, then close the implemented dogfooding issues.
invariants: Planning state stays clean and machine-readable.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Broad/high-assurance package work needs checked-in planning before implementation.
Archive closeout errors should name actionable field paths.
Archive size policy should fail before writing oversized archives.
open_questions_to_close: Whether #651/#652/#653 can land in the same PR after workflow/archive robustness or need narrower child issues.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py tests/test_generated_tool_conformance.py tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py -q
required_tools: node if generated adapter conformance is changed
completion_criteria: Named dogfooding issues are implemented and closed, or explicitly routed with narrower follow-ups.
Focused proof and make check pass.
Active execplan is archived and planning state is clean.

execution_run.scope touched: Planning summary/projection, archive-plan validation, focused tests, and active execplan.
execution_run.changed surfaces: Planning state, execplan, planning bootstrap installer, and workspace CLI tests.
execution_run.validations run: uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "next_step_and_warns or required_traceability_ref_paths or oversized_archive or broad_work_planning_guard or adaptive_assurance_end_to_end"; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py -q
execution_run.result for continuation: #645, #649, #650, #651, #652, #653, #654, and #655 implemented locally with full proof.
execution_run.next step: Commit and push the generated-output and conformance/modeling batch, then close the implemented dogfooding issues.

finished_run_review.config compliance: active checked-in execplan created before implementation
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: medium until issue batching is complete

proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: make check passed after focused planning, contract, generated package, conformance, and structured inventory checks.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve larger outcomes and checked-in planning for work whose proof or continuation would be expensive to reconstruct.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Use progressive maturity and explicit continuation surfaces.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this lane reduce workflow friction without adding unnecessary process overhead?

closure_check.reopen trigger: Focused proof or issue review finds unsatisfied acceptance.

generated_closeout.authority: derived adapter; structured execplan fields remain authoritative

memory_learning_capture.reason: Route only if a reusable learning is not already captured by issues/checks.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.

drift_log: 2026-05-01: Created active dogfooding robustness execplan after #641 merge.
2026-05-01: Implemented workflow/archive robustness batch for #645, #649, #654, and #655; focused planning tests passed.
2026-05-01: Implemented generated-output and conformance/modeling batch for #650, #651, #652, and #653; make check passed.
